lines.content: * Constructs an AddCommand with the specified description and amount.
* @param amount Monetary value of the expense.
* Executes the add command by creating an Expense and adding it to the list.
* @param expenseList The list where the expense will be stored.
System.out.println("Got it. I've added this expense:");
System.out.println("Now you have " + expenseList.getSize() + " expense(s) in the list.");
* Represents a single financial expense tracked by the user.
* Contains the description and the monetary amount of the expense.
* Constructs an Expense object with the specified description and amount.
* @param description The details or name of the expense.
* @param amount The monetary cost of the expense.
* Retrieves the description of the expense.
* Retrieves the monetary amount of the expense.
* Returns a string representation of the expense, formatting the amount to two decimal places.
* @return A formatted string showing the description and cost.
* Handles operations such as adding, deleting, and retrieving expenses from the list.
* Adds a new expense to the end of the list.
* @param expense The Expense object to be added.
* Returns the current number of expenses in the list.
* @return The size of the expense list.
* Retrieves an expense from the list based on its index.
* @param index The zero-based index of the expense to retrieve.
* Removes an expense from the list based on its index.
* @param index The zero-based index of the expense to be deleted.
* The main entry point for the SpendSwift application.
* Initializes the application and starts the interaction loop with the user.
* Constructs a SpendSwift instance and initializes the core components.
* Loads any previously saved expenses from disk.
* Runs the main loop of the application.
* Continuously reads user input and handles the "exit" command.
System.out.println("What expenses are we tracking today?");
System.out.println("Amount must be a valid number.");
System.out.println("Bye. Hope to see you again soon!");
System.out.println("Unknown command. Type 'help' to see available commands.");
* Prints the help menu showing all available commands and their formats.
System.out.println("  add AMOUNT DESCRIPTION  - Add a new expense");
System.out.println("  delete INDEX            - Delete an expense by index");
System.out.println("  help                    - Show this help menu");
* Prints all current expenses to the user
System.out.println("Your expense list is currently empty.");
* The main method that launches the SpendSwift application.
* Handles reading and writing expense data to a text file
* so that data persists between sessions.
* Constructs a Storage object with the given file path.
* @param filePath The path to the data file.
* Loads expenses from the data file into the given ExpenseList.
* If the file does not exist, an empty list is returned.
* @param expenseList The ExpenseList to populate with saved data.
System.out.println("Warning: Could not load data file. Starting with empty list.");
* Saves all expenses from the given ExpenseList to the data file.
* Creates the parent directory if it does not exist.
* @param expenseList The ExpenseList whose data should be saved.
System.out.println("Warning: Could not save data to file.");
* Parses a single line from the data file into an Expense object.
* @param line The line to parse, in the format "AMOUNT | DESCRIPTION".
* @return The parsed Expense, or null if the line is malformed.
System.out.println("Warning: Skipping line with invalid amount: " + line);
